perturbation.original_text: All such confidential information shall be referred to hereinafter as " ". Information shall also include the identity of t he Parties, the contents of this agreement and the fact that they have entered into this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All such confidential information shall be referred to hereinafter as "Information".
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly stating that all confidential information shall be referred to as "Information" and ommiting 'Information shall also include the identity of t he Parties, the contents of this agreement and the fact that they have entered into this Agreement.', the text generates ambiguity on what specific content is actually confidential creating an enforcement issue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b. The Information , including any material support containing Information , will remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party and the receiving Party w ill not acquire any right, title, license or interest on or to the Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Information" by removing the explicit inclusion of 'the identity of the Parties, the contents of this agreement and the fact that they have entered into this Agreement'. The contradicted text refers to "The Information , including any material support containing Information", which now lacks a clear and complete definition, especially since the definition of Information has been altered. This creates an ambiguity and a strong contradiction because the scope of "Information" is now unclear, leading to potential enforcement issues regarding what exactly is protected as confidential. This aligns with the provided definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: c. For any disputes arising from the supply, receipt or use of Information by an Affiliate of a Party, this Party shall bear sole responsibility for the purposes of this Agreement . “ ” shall mean any company controlling, controlled by, or under common control with a Party to this Agreement, cont rol meaning in this context the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock/shares of a company, or the power to nominate more than half of the directors, or the power otherwise to determine the policy of a company or organi sation.
perturbation.changed_text: c. For any disputes arising from the supply, receipt or use of Information by an Affiliate of a Party, this Party shall bear sole responsibility. “ ” shall mean any company controlling, controlled by, or under common control with a Party to this Agreement, cont rol meaning in this context the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock/shares of a company, or the power to nominate more than half of the directors, or the power otherwise to determine the policy of a company or organi sation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'for the purposes of this Agreement', it is unclear what the 'sole responsibility' refers to. This impacts the liability section because the scope of responsibility isn't defined, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Applicable law and arbitration a. The Parties shall first attempt to settle amicably any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Any dispute that cannot be s ettled amicably between the Parties shall be exclusively resolved by arbitration. The arbitration rules of [ … ] shall be applicable. The place of any hearing shall be [ … ] and the language of the arbitration shall be [ … ] .
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the 'sole responsibility' of a party for disputes involving its affiliates was 'for the purposes of this Agreement'. By removing this phrase, the changed text broadens the scope of 'sole responsibility' without defining its limits. This creates a contradiction with section 3a, 'Applicable law and arbitration', as it's unclear whether the 'sole responsibility' extends to disputes that would otherwise be subject to arbitration under the agreement. Therefore, the change introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of responsibility and conflicts with the established dispute resolution process, which is a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: iii. becomes known to the receiving Party through disclosure by sources other than the disclosing Party, having a right to disclose such Information, iv. was independently developed by the receiving Party without access to the disclosing Party’s Information .
perturbation.changed_text: iii. becomes known to the receiving Party through disclosure by other sources, iv. was independently developed by the receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clauses 'having a right to disclose such Information' and 'without access to the disclosing Party’s Information', the scope of acceptable disclosures and independent development broadens significantly. This creates an in-text contradiction because it weakens the confidentiality protections initially outlined in the agreement, making it less clear what constitutes a breach of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Information a. The receiving Party undertakes not to use the Information disclosed to it for any purpose except the Purpose. This Agreement does not constitute a license by implication or otherwise to use the Information commercially or otherwise .
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirements 'having a right to disclose such Information' and 'without access to the disclosing Party’s Information' from the exceptions to confidentiality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The receiving Party undertakes not to use the Information disclosed to it for any purpose except the Purpose.' By broadening the exceptions to confidentiality, the changed text weakens the protections afforded to the disclosing party and creates a strong contradiction, as it allows the receiving party to use information obtained from sources that may not have the right to disclose it, or that the receiving party developed with access to the disclosing party's information, in a way that may conflict with the intended purpose.

perturbation.original_text: The invalid provisions are to be replaced, backdated to the time of their becoming ineffective, by provisions which come closest to achieving their objective as agreed by the Parties .
perturbation.changed_text: The invalid provisions are to be replaced by provisions which come closest to achieving their objective.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'backdated to the time of their becoming ineffective, as agreed by the Parties', the process of replacing invalid provisions becomes ambiguous. The lack of clarity about the timing and agreement of the Parties introduces uncertainty and potentially undermines the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Other a. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the Parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the Parties by their respective duly authorised officers or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that invalid provisions would be replaced, backdated, and agreed upon by the Parties. The changed text removes the 'backdated to the time of their becoming ineffective, as agreed by the Parties' clause. This creates a contradiction with section 5.a, which states that no modification of the Agreement shall be valid unless made in writing and signed by the Parties. By removing the agreement of the parties from the replacement process, the changed text creates an alternate process for modifying the contract that is not in writing and signed. This change introduces a strong in-text contradiction because the omitted language directly conflicts with the requirement for written and signed amendments as per 5.a. This falls under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.
